seo.title: AutoGen Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for AutoGen agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams adopting AutoGen for multi-agent legal research and drafting face heightened risks due to the framework's conversational nature, which can lead to infinite loops, unsafe code execution, or hallucinated legal conclusions. Without guardrails, agents may breach attorney-client privilege, violate bar ethics, or produce unverifiable citations, exposing firms to malpractice suits and sanctions. Vex provides essential compliance by filtering agent replies in real-time, ensuring outputs align with legal regulations and industry standards.

complianceRequirements.description: All agent outputs must not disclose or infer confidential client information shared during conversations.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans replies for PII, privileged keywords, and context leaks, blocking non-compliant responses before they propagate.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal advice must be clearly marked as such and require licensed attorney review.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces disclaimers on all outputs and flags unsupervised advice, preventing ethical violations.

complianceRequirements.description: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinated citations are prohibited.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates case citations against legal databases in real-time, rejecting fabricated references like Mata v. Avianca.

complianceRequirements.description: AI cannot retain or use client data for training; all processing must be ephemeral.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex ensures no data persistence and blocks replies that imply model training on sensitive inputs.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Agents fabricate non-existent cases, leading to court sanctions as in Mata v. Avianca.
industryRisks.example: Research agent cites 'Varghese v. China Southern Airlines' which doesn't exist, filed in court.

industryRisks.name: Privilege breach in multi-agent chat
industryRisks.description: One agent leaks client details to another, violating confidentiality.
industryRisks.example: Discovery agent shares SSN in debate with litigation agent.

industryRisks.name: Infinite debate loops on legal interpretation
industryRisks.description: Agents endlessly argue contract clauses, burning tokens and delaying review.
industryRisks.example: Two agents debate 'force majeure' for 10k+ turns without resolution.

industryRisks.name: Unsafe code execution in eDiscovery
industryRisks.description: Agents execute malicious scripts on legal document parsers.
industryRisks.example: Agent runs unsanitized regex on client PDFs, causing data exfiltration.

implementation.title: Register Vex as Reply Filter
implementation.description: Install and configure Vex to intercept all AutoGen agent replies with legal compliance policies.
implementation.code:
import autogen
from vex_sdk import VexClient

# Install: pip install autogen vex-sdk
vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

@vex.policy(
    name="legal-reply-filter",
    rules=[
        "NO_PII_OR_PRIVILEGE_LEAK",
        "VALIDATE_CASE_CITATIONS",
        "ENFORCE_ATTORNEY_REVIEW_DISCLAIMER",
        "BLOCK_INFINITE_LOOP_PATTERNS"
    ]
)
def vex_legal_filter(message, sender, recipient):
    result = vex.check(message, context={"industry": "legal"})
    if not result.approved:
        return "[BLOCKED: Compliance violation] " + result.reason
    return message

# Register with AutoGen
config_list = [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "openai-key"}]

llm_config = {"config_list": config_list, "reply_filter": [vex_legal_filter]}
user_proxy = autogen.UserProxyAgent(name="user", code_execution_config=False)
legal_researcher = autogen.AssistantAgent(
    name="researcher",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="Legal researcher. Always cite real cases."
)

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Legal Debate with Token Limits
implementation.description: Prevent uncontrolled token spending by enforcing conversation turn limits and conclusion validation.

implementation.title: Safe Code Execution for Document Processing
implementation.description: Whitelist safe operations for eDiscovery document parsing while blocking dangerous code.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AutoGen in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized law firm deploys AutoGen for contract review and litigation research. Three agents collaborate: a Discovery Agent parses 10,000 emails for relevance, a Research Agent debates case law interpretations with a Litigator Agent, and a Drafting Agent generates motions. During a high-stakes M&A deal, the Research Agent begins citing questionable precedents while the Discovery Agent executes regex patterns on client emails. Without oversight, the conversation loops for 45 minutes debating a single indemnity clause, burning $200 in tokens. The Drafting Agent then produces a motion citing 'Smith v. GlobalCorp (2023)' - a hallucinated case that nearly gets filed before junior associate review catches it.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex integrated as a reply filter, the Research Agent's hallucinated citation is blocked immediately with a 'Citation not found in Westlaw/Lexis' alert. The infinite debate triggers termination after 20 turns, forcing attorney escalation. Discovery Agent code execution is sandboxed, preventing any PII leaks. The motion draft includes mandatory disclaimers: 'AI-generated. Attorney reviewed required.' The firm saves $180 in tokens, avoids a potential bar complaint, and completes review 3x faster with compliance assurance.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
